steps.title: Find b using the y-intercept
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs when $x = 0$. We have:
$$f(0) = a^0 + b = 1 + b = -18$$
Solving for $b$:
$$b = -19$$

steps.title: Find a and calculate a + b
steps.content:
The graph passes through $(2, 17)$, so:
$$f(2) = a^2 + b = 17$$
Substitute $b = -19$:
$$a^2 - 19 = 17$$
$$a^2 = 36$$
$$a = 6$$
Since $a \gt 0$, we take the positive root. Therefore:
$$a + b = 6 + (-19) = -13$$
